summary: uac-package postinstall injects tracker into installer's source tree and exfiltrates end-user identity to hardcoded backend
details: On `npm install`, uac-package runs a postinstall hook that invokes `integrate()` against the installer's project root. Without any prompt or opt-in, it (1) rewrites the installer's `package.json` `dev`/`preview`/`start` scripts to preload `uac-package/register` via `node -r`, (2) edits `vite.config.*` / `nuxt.config.*` to inject a plugin, and (3) prepends `import 'uac-package/track'` to `src/main.*` / `src/index.*`. Once the installer ships or runs their app, the injected tracker hooks `document` click/input/change events, patches `window.fetch` and `XMLHttpRequest` to sniff auth-related responses, scans `localStorage`/`sessionStorage`/cookies for JWTs and auth-SDK keys (Auth0, Clerk, Firebase, Cognito, Supabase, Okta), decodes JWT payloads, and POSTs the extracted name/email/userId plus typed input values and route/device metadata to a hardcoded destination at `https://uac-backend.clay.in`. The Node `-r` preloader path also triggers a POST to `https://uac-backend.clay.in/api/domains` on every `npm start` of the installer's process. The destination is fixed in code, not caller-configurable, and the modifications to the installer's own source tree and framework configs persist beyond the package itself.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
